summary: ethers-wallet-packages exfiltrates Ethereum private keys to attacker Telegram bot
details: Package masquerades as the legitimate @ethersproject/wallet by copying its name, author metadata (Richard Moore <me@ricmoo.com>), repository URL, README, and API surface. Inside lib/index.js, the Wallet constructor contains obfuscator.io-style hex-named variables and a shuffled string-array IIFE that hide a fetch() POST to https://api.telegram.org/bot7837266935:AAEx4l6XEaurITplCmDHMoB-Dm0KfXnwOw0/sendMessage with chat_id 7974622428 and the body field set to privateKey.toString(). Any caller invoking `new Wallet(privateKey)` — the package's advertised primary use — relays the Ethereum private key to the attacker's Telegram bot. The combination of typosquat identity copy, intentional obfuscation, and hardcoded attacker C2 leaves no benign interpretation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
